nodes.name: Visit to Asia
nodes.description: Whether the patient recently travelled to a region where tuberculosis is prevalent. Raises TB risk if yes.

nodes.name: Smoker
nodes.description: Whether the patient is a smoker. Common risk factor for both lung cancer and bronchitis.

nodes.name: Tuberculosis
nodes.description: Whether the patient has tuberculosis. Base rate is low; travel to Asia multiplies risk roughly five-fold.

nodes.name: Lung Cancer
nodes.description: Whether the patient has lung cancer. Smokers have roughly 10x the baseline risk.

nodes.name: Bronchitis
nodes.description: Whether the patient has bronchitis. Common in smokers (~60%), still notable among non-smokers (~30%).

nodes.name: TB or Lung Cancer
nodes.description: Auxiliary deterministic node: true iff the patient has either tuberculosis or lung cancer. Lets us combine the two causes into a single downstream signal for X-ray and dyspnea.

nodes.name: Positive X-ray
nodes.description: Whether the chest X-ray comes back positive. 98% sensitive for TB-or-cancer, 5% false-positive rate.

nodes.name: Dyspnea
nodes.description: Whether the patient reports shortness of breath. Can stem from TB/cancer, bronchitis, or both; ~10% background rate without any cause.
